items.title: GitHub MCP Push to Private Repos — PERMANENTLY BLOCKED (Cancelled)
items.description:
CANCELLED 2026-06-17 — PERMANENT ARCHITECTURAL LIMITATION.

The GitHub MCP connector (OAuth via api.githubcopilot.com/mcp) uses the Copilot OAuth app which requests public_repo scope only. Even with a full repo-scope PAT, the OAuth app's scope cap means ALL write operations (push_files, create_or_update_file) return HTTP 403 'Resource not accessible by integration' on private repos. This is NOT fixable by re-granting or changing token settings — it is a hard Copilot OAuth app limitation.

APPROVED WORKAROUNDS (use these instead):
1. PRIVATE REPO WRITES: Use PowerShell push script eng-agent/push_skills_to_github.ps1 with $env:GITHUB_PAT set to a fine-grained PAT (Contents read/write on trading-bot-v3). Command: powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File 'eng-agent/push_skills_to_github.ps1'
2. SINGLE-FILE WRITES: Use Chrome web UI (GitHub.com editor). Confirmed working for README.md, CHANGELOG.md, dashboard files.
3. PUBLIC REPO READS: GitHub MCP works fine for reading public repos (trading-bot-dashboard).

ENGINEERING RULE: Never attempt GitHub MCP push_files or create_or_update_file on private repos in ANY routine or agent session. This wastes cycles and logs 403 errors. AI-CONTEXT.md Section 'GitHub Access Methods' documents this permanently. This issue has been repeatedly rediscovered — this cancellation + note is the final record.
items.acceptance_criteria: One successful automated full-set push via GitHub MCP. N=2 failure escalation tested.
items.queue_note: QUEUED TO JASHYARD — no agent can execute; awaiting your action/decision.
items.notes: 2026-06-13 finding: official GitHub MCP (api.githubcopilot.com/mcp) connects + READS fine as JashyardEngDev75, but WRITES return 403 "Resource not accessible by integration" on BOTH the contents API (create_or_update_file) AND the git-trees API (push_files) - even after re-grant + full reconnect. Conclusion: the hosted Copilot-backed GitHub MCP issues a READ-ONLY token for this account; repo-permission toggles do not lift it. RESOLUTION OPTIONS: (A) keep MCP for reads, publish via Chrome web-editor (already working, R2/R7 fallback) - zero setup; (B) for MCP-native writes, use a fine-grained PAT (Contents R/W on both repos) with a local/self-hosted github-mcp-server or PAT custom connector (Jashyard creates the token). RM-003 stays In Progress; dashboard publishing is NOT blocked (Chrome path live).
items.cancel_reason: GitHub MCP OAuth scope permanently blocks private repo writes. Workarounds documented. See AI-CONTEXT.md GitHub Access Methods section.

items.title: [STRATEGIC] Market-Hours Uptime Heartbeat + Page-on-Down
items.description: ROOT-CAUSE FIX for the #1 PnL-relevant failure mode. The 06-12 zero-entry day was NOT a code bug: the Cowork app was CLOSED during market hours, so R5/R6 deferred and fired post-window. A Sunday-5pm prep check was added, but there is NO in-window detection that the app/routines are actually live. This item adds a lightweight heartbeat that confirms each entry routine fired within its window on each open day and pages Jashyard (Slack + email) FAST if a window is missed (detect within minutes, not at EOD). Distinct from RM-074 (failover) and RM-061 (simplify schedule): this is an uptime SLA + alerting layer. Directly accelerates Phase 1 because every missed favorable session is a lost scored signal toward the 30-signal gate.
items.source: Jordan Strategic Session 2026-06-13 — R8 INS-001 + 06-12 root cause
items.acceptance_criteria: A heartbeat checks, per open day, that each entry routine (R1/R3/R4/R5/R6) logged a start within ±10 min of its window; a missed window posts a Slack alert + email to Jashyard within 15 min of the window close; verified by a drill that simulates a missed window. No engine/scoring/gate change.

items.source: Jordan Strategic Session 2026-06-13 — R8 research_question (5 email_failures)

items.title: Monte Carlo Regime-Blocked Bootstrap Fix
items.description: The Monte Carlo gate uses an IID assumption that is violated in the current dataset: with N=4 trades (all from the same DOW, ticker, window, and market regime), the MC simulation produces false precision. IID bootstrap treats each trade as independent when they are structurally correlated. This inflates confidence in the DSR/MC going-live gate and could allow promotion with insufficient evidence. Fix: implement regime-blocked or stationary bootstrap that preserves the time-series structure of trades. Added 2026-06-15 from PM triage / KPI audit finding.
items.source: 2026-06-15 PM triage - KPI/data audit finding; IID assumption violates MC bootstrap validity
items.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN current IID bootstrap in live_gate_stats.py MC gate, WHEN regime-blocked bootstrap is implemented, THEN MC samples are drawn in blocks that preserve auto-correlation structure of the trade sequence, confidence interval width increases appropriately (wider CI = more conservative), AND the gate does not produce a false PASS at N < 10 natural signals. OOS validation required before deploying new bootstrap method.
items.queue_note: Blocked until N >= 10 natural signals exist (can't validate a bootstrap fix on N=0 data). Do not deploy until RM-001 Phase 1 is complete and signals are accruing. Jashyard approval required before any gate logic change.

items.title: Forbidden Account Proactive Daily Scan
items.description: Current enforcement of the forbidden account rule (never reference/touch forbidden accounts) is runtime-only at trade execution. There is no proactive audit that scans logs, config files, or recent outputs for any mention of forbidden account numbers. A daily scan would catch any agent drift, accidental logging, or config corruption before it reaches trade execution. This is a low-effort P1 security guard. Added 2026-06-15 from PM triage security review.
items.source: 2026-06-15 PM triage - proactive security gap identified
items.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN forbidden account numbers must never appear in logs, config, or outputs, WHEN daily scan routine runs (suggested: part of R1 integrity check), THEN it scans all log files and config in LOGS_DIR for the forbidden account strings, reports CLEAN or ALERT in Slack #trading-bot-notifications, and on ALERT: posts to Slack with file + line reference and halts until Jashyard clears it. Scan must complete in < 10 seconds.
items.queue_note: Low effort, high-value security item. Can be added to existing R1 integrity block. Does not require Jashyard approval to implement (security enforcement, not strategy change) - but output format/alert threshold should be confirmed.

items.title: Recommendation Lifecycle State Machine
items.description: apply_recommendation.py has no formal state machine for recommendations. REC-20260613-01, REC-20260613-02, and REC-20260613-03 are stuck at PROPOSED with no automated tracking of who reviewed them or what decision was made. A recommendation lifecycle state machine would enforce: PROPOSED -> UNDER_REVIEW -> APPROVED/REJECTED -> APPLIED/CLOSED, with timestamps and reviewer recorded at each transition. Prevents recommendation rot where valid suggestions languish indefinitely. Added 2026-06-15 from PM triage.
items.source: 2026-06-15 PM triage - REC-20260613-01/02/03 all stuck at PROPOSED
items.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN recommendations currently have no enforced lifecycle, WHEN state machine is implemented in apply_recommendation.py, THEN each recommendation has a status field (PROPOSED/UNDER_REVIEW/APPROVED/REJECTED/APPLIED/CLOSED), each transition records a timestamp and actor, R8 analytics surfaces any recommendation stuck in PROPOSED > 14 days, AND Jashyard receives a weekly digest of pending recommendations via Slack.
items.queue_note: P2 - important for governance but not on the live-path critical chain. Can be worked in parallel with P0/P1 items. Jashyard approval needed before implementing state transitions that could auto-close recommendations.

Bar-estimated EMA fallback is running non-blocking, but fallback accuracy vs. native FMP EMA is unknown. Decision needed: (1) root-cause the FMP endpoint failure and fix, or (2) replace with bar-computed EMA permanently as primary path. Added from Jashyard #trading-bot-ideas post at 09:40 MST 2026-06-15.
items.source: 2026-06-15 Jashyard #trading-bot-ideas 09:40 MST - FMP EMA-3 10 consecutive timeouts
items.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the FMP technicalIndicators EMA-3/5min endpoint has been timing out, WHEN the fix is implemented, THEN: (1) bar-computed EMA is promoted as primary path in signal_engine.py, (2) FMP EMA is attempted as optional enrichment when available (non-blocking), (3) signal_engine.py logs ema_source (bar_computed or fmp) per scan, (4) accuracy delta documented in TECH_NOTES.md, AND (5) R1 integrity check verifies EMA source on startup.

FMP EMA becomes optional enrichment (attempt on each scan, non-blocking, log when available). Create a structured folder organization standard and an automated cleanup routine that runs weekly to archive old logs, delete temp files, enforce folder structure, and report what was cleaned to Slack.
items.source: 2026-06-15 Jashyard #trading-bot-ideas - folder organization and automated cleanup
items.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN folder structure has grown organically, WHEN cleanup is implemented, THEN: (1) defined folder structure is documented in TECH_NOTES.md, (2) weekly cleanup routine runs Saturday and posts summary to Slack, (3) logs older than 30 days are archived (not deleted) to /archive subfolder, AND (4) cleanup routine never touches live log files that routines depend on.
items.queue_note: Low priority quality-of-life. Defer until P0/P1 items are resolved. Cleanup must whitelist critical files (logs/, roadmap.json, AI-CONTEXT.md, etc.).

items.title: Unit Testing Framework for Signal Engine and Core Modules
items.description: The signal engine and core bot modules (signal_engine.py, live_gate_stats.py, apply_recommendation.py) have no automated tests. Any change risks silent regressions. Implement a pytest unit testing framework with signal engine output tests against known input snapshots, gate logic boundary tests, log schema validation tests, and a CI-style pre-push test run.
items.source: 2026-06-15 Jashyard #trading-bot-ideas - unit testing framework before going live
items.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN no automated tests exist for core modules, WHEN framework is implemented, THEN: (1) pytest suite covers signal engine with at least 10 input/output snapshot tests, (2) gate boundary conditions are tested, (3) log schema tests validate required fields on all log writes, (4) tests run in <60 seconds, AND (5) all tests pass on current codebase before any new RM item is implemented.
items.queue_note: Should be implemented before P0 items are deployed to reduce regression risk. Pairs with RM-114 (reliability scorecard). Consider making test suite passage a gate for RM deployment.

items.title: Comprehensive Security Audit - 80/20 Approach
items.description: Conduct an 80/20 security audit focused on highest-risk surfaces: credential exposure in logs and public repo, PAT and API key lifecycle, Robinhood account access controls (FORBIDDEN accounts), public dashboard data scrubbing, and Slack webhook security. Goal: a focused sweep of the most likely failure vectors before going live.
items.source: 2026-06-15 Jashyard #trading-bot-ideas - comprehensive security audit 80/20 before going live
items.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN no formal security audit has been conducted, WHEN audit is complete, THEN: (1) all log files are scanned for credential leakage, (2) public repo is verified to contain no strategy params or credentials, (3) PAT rotation schedule is documented, (4) forbidden account references (----, ----) are confirmed absent from all code paths, AND (5) audit findings are documented in a report for Jashyard review.
items.queue_note: Must complete before live trading flip. Focus on credential hygiene first. Pairs with RM-123 and RM-128. Morgan (Security) leads with Rachel support.

items.title: Factor Score Logging on All Gate-Blocked Signal Attempts
items.description: From 06/13 Research Drop (3-agent convergence): when a signal attempt is blocked by any gate, the individual factor scores that contributed to the block are not logged. This makes post-hoc analysis of why a signal was blocked impossible. Log the full factor score breakdown (RSI, VIX, gap, momentum scores) on every gate-blocked attempt in signal_attempts.jsonl with a blocked_reason field.
items.source: 2026-06-13 Weekly Research Drop #1 - 3-agent convergence: factor score logging on blocked attempts
items.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN gate-blocked attempts have no factor score record, WHEN logging is implemented, THEN: (1) every gate-blocked signal attempt includes a factor_scores object with individual component scores, (2) a blocked_reason field lists which specific gate(s) blocked the attempt, (3) the field is present even when all gates pass (for comparison), AND (4) analytics can compute factor score distributions across blocked vs. passed attempts.
items.queue_note: P0 - unbackfillable data loss. Every day without this logging is signal insight we can never recover. Implement before next market session.

items.title: Error Taxonomy - Mandatory Error Type Tagging in errors.jsonl
items.description: From 06/13 Research Drop: errors.jsonl entries have inconsistent formats with no standardized error_type taxonomy. Define a mandatory error_type taxonomy (API_TIMEOUT, ENGINE_INTEGRITY_FAILURE, GATE_BLOCK, FORBIDDEN_ACCOUNT, LOG_WRITE_FAILURE, etc.) and enforce it on all errors.jsonl writes. Add a schema validation step to the engine startup check.
items.source: 2026-06-13 Weekly Research Drop #2 - error taxonomy mandatory tagging in errors.jsonl
items.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN errors.jsonl has no standardized error_type field, WHEN taxonomy is implemented, THEN: (1) canonical error_type enum is documented in TECH_NOTES.md, (2) all errors.jsonl writes include a mandatory error_type field from the enum, (3) unknown error types cause a validation warning, (4) engine startup check validates recent errors.jsonl entries for schema compliance, AND (5) migration script tags existing entries with best-guess error_type values.
items.queue_note: P0 foundational logging hygiene. Required for RM-109 (signal drought telemetry) and automated alerting on error patterns. Low effort, high value.

items.title: Engine Integrity Sentinel - Pre-Exec Check + Auto-Restore
items.description: From 06/13 Research Drop: the engine integrity check (R1 step) currently logs failures but does not block execution or attempt auto-restore. Implement an Engine Integrity Sentinel that: (1) runs before any trading routine, (2) checks signal_engine.py SHA-256 against MANIFEST.json pins, (3) if mismatch: blocks routine, posts Slack alert, attempts auto-restore from GitHub, (4) if auto-restore fails: hard-stop with HOLD status.
items.source: 2026-06-13 Weekly Research Drop #3 - engine integrity sentinel with auto-restore
items.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN integrity check currently logs but does not block, WHEN sentinel is implemented, THEN: (1) every routine begins with SHA-256 integrity check against MANIFEST.json, (2) mismatch blocks the routine immediately and posts HOLD alert to #trading-bot-notifications, (3) auto-restore from GitHub is attempted and logged, (4) if restore fails, routine is cancelled with clear error message, AND (5) test with deliberately corrupted engine validates sentinel behavior.
items.queue_note: DONE 2026-06-17: Step 2b SHA-256 sentinel inserted into SKILL-r0-premarket-qa.md. Blocks routine on engine hash mismatch, attempts self_heal.py auto-restore, hard-stop on failure. R2 force-close exempt per Dana's amendment.

items.title: VIX Proxy Calibration Diagnostic / H-VIXDECAY Daily Monitor
items.description: From 06/13 Research Drop: the H-VIXDECAY hypothesis (VIX overestimates next-day realized vol, creating a systematic short-vol edge) has not been validated against recent data. The VIX gate uses a fixed threshold (30) set without calibration. Implement a daily H-VIXDECAY diagnostic that logs VIX vs. next-day realized vol and computes the running decay ratio. Feeds into RM-129 (VIX gate blended threshold).
items.source: 2026-06-13 Weekly Research Drop #4 - VIX proxy calibration diagnostic / H-VIXDECAY monitor
items.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN VIX gate threshold is uncalibrated, WHEN diagnostic is implemented, THEN: (1) daily log records VIX at session open vs. next-day realized volatility, (2) running decay ratio is computed over trailing 20 sessions, (3) decay ratio below 1.0 for 5+ consecutive sessions triggers a recalibration review alert, AND (4) data feeds directly into RM-129 (blended vol threshold design).
items.queue_note: Pairs with RM-129. The H-VIXDECAY hypothesis is a core edge assumption - needs ongoing empirical validation, not a set-and-forget threshold.

items.title: Scheduler Window Truth-Table Validator
items.description: From 06/13 Research Drop (extends RM-047): the scheduler has no mechanism to validate that all expected routine windows fired within the session. Implement a truth-table validator that at EOD checks: did R1 fire? R2? R3-R7? If any expected window was missed, post a miss-report to Slack with the reason. Directly addresses R5 25% and R7 0% success rate anomalies.
items.source: 2026-06-13 Weekly Research Drop #5 - scheduler truth-table validator, extends RM-047
items.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN R5 and R7 have anomalously low success rates, WHEN validator is implemented, THEN: (1) EOD routine checks session_log for each expected R1-R7 window, (2) any missed window is flagged in a miss-report posted to #trading-bot-notifications, (3) miss-reports include last-known-run timestamp and inferred reason where possible, AND (4) validator runs even on market-closed days.
items.queue_note: Directly addresses R5/R7 reliability mystery. Low effort relative to value. Pairs with RM-093 (scheduled task priority audit).

items.title: DSR De-Hardcode + Trial Ledger Bootstrap
items.description: From 06/13 Research Drop #6 and 06/15 Drop #3 (convergence): the Dynamic Signal Requirement (DSR) threshold is hardcoded at 10 in the signal engine. It should be computed from trial_ledger.json, which tracks performance by regime. Additionally, the trial_ledger is never actually read by gate logic despite being logged. Fix: (1) de-hardcode DSR, (2) bootstrap trial ledger from historical paper trades, (3) wire trial_ledger reads into gate computation.
items.source: 2026-06-13 Research Drop #6 + 2026-06-15 Research Drop #3 - DSR hardcode + trial ledger bootstrap
items.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN DSR is hardcoded at 10 and trial_ledger is never read, WHEN fix is implemented, THEN: (1) DSR is computed from trial_ledger with hardcoded=10 as fallback when ledger is empty, (2) trial_ledger bootstrap populates from existing paper trade history, (3) gate logic reads trial_ledger on each scan, (4) DSR value and source (computed vs. fallback) is logged per scan, AND (5) OOS validation shows computed DSR produces equivalent or better gate decisions.
items.queue_note: P1 gate integrity issue. DSR=10 hardcode is a bias-to-pass risk flagged in the KPI audit. A gate condition that is logged but never evaluated is worse than no condition.

items.title: Session Funnel / Signal Drought Telemetry
items.description: From 06/13 Research Drop #7 and 06/15 Drop #5 (convergence): no telemetry exists for the signal generation funnel within a session. Cannot answer: how many scans ran? How many passed RSI? VIX? All gates? This funnel data is critical for diagnosing signal drought (0 natural signals over 6 models). Implement per-session funnel logging that records scan counts at each gate stage.
items.source: 2026-06-13 Research Drop #7 + 2026-06-15 Research Drop #5 - session funnel / signal drought telemetry
items.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN no per-session funnel data exists, WHEN telemetry is implemented, THEN: (1) each session log includes a funnel object with scan_count, rsi_pass_count, vix_pass_count, gap_pass_count, all_gates_pass_count, trades_executed, (2) funnel data is posted in the R7 EOD Slack summary, (3) funnel data feeds the signal drought monitor, AND (4) dashboard shows 7-day funnel trend chart.
items.queue_note: P1 diagnostic. Without funnel data we are flying blind on where signals die. Foundation for all gate calibration work.

items.title: Feed Integrity Blocking Gate
items.description: From 06/13 Research Drop #8: the signal engine continues scanning even when FMP data quality is suspect (missing bars, stale quotes, API errors). A bad data feed can produce false signals. Implement a feed integrity blocking gate that validates bar count and recency on each FMP pull, checks for duplicate timestamps, and blocks signal generation (not just logs a warning) if data quality falls below threshold.
items.source: 2026-06-13 Weekly Research Drop #8 - feed integrity blocking gate
items.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN signal engine continues on bad FMP data, WHEN gate is implemented, THEN: (1) each FMP bar pull is validated for expected bar count, recency (last bar within N minutes of scan time), and no duplicate timestamps, (2) validation failure blocks signal generation and logs FEED_INTEGRITY_FAILURE, (3) Slack alert is posted with failure details, AND (4) gate is configurable per instrument.
items.queue_note: P1 safety gate. Garbage in, garbage trades out. Must ship before adding any new instruments (RM-099).

items.title: RSI Boundary Direction Conflict Flag
items.description: From 06/13 Research Drop #9 and 06/15 Drop #19 (convergence): the RSI signal logic may produce boundary conflicts where RSI is near 50 (neutral zone) but the model votes a directional entry. Implement a conflict flag that detects when RSI is in the neutral band (45-55 configurable), logs an rsi_conflict field on the signal attempt, and applies a configurable confidence penalty.
items.source: 2026-06-13 Research Drop #9 + 2026-06-15 Research Drop #19 - RSI boundary direction conflict
items.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN RSI neutral-zone conflicts are undetected, WHEN flag is implemented, THEN: (1) rsi_conflict field is logged on all signal attempts where RSI is in neutral band, (2) neutral band boundaries are configurable (default 45-55), (3) confidence penalty is applied to conflict attempts (default -10%), AND (4) analytics can compute conflict rate and impact on signal quality before any hard-blocking behavior is enabled.
items.queue_note: P2 signal quality improvement. Log first, analyze, then optionally block. Do not hard-block without data showing conflict attempts have lower win rates.

items.title: Forbidden Account Audit Trail in errors.jsonl
items.description: From 06/13 Research Drop #10: if the bot ever attempts to interact with the FORBIDDEN accounts (---- or ----), the only protection is the code guard with no audit trail. Implement: any code path that would touch a forbidden account instead logs a FORBIDDEN_ACCOUNT_ATTEMPT error to errors.jsonl, sends an immediate Slack alert to #trading-bot-notifications, and hard-blocks the attempt with no retry.
items.source: 2026-06-13 Weekly Research Drop #10 - forbidden account audit trail
items.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN forbidden account access has no audit trail, WHEN logging is implemented, THEN: (1) any reference to forbidden account IDs in a live code path triggers a FORBIDDEN_ACCOUNT_ATTEMPT log entry in errors.jsonl, (2) a Slack alert fires immediately with account ID and calling code location, (3) the attempt is hard-blocked with no order placed, AND (4) a test scenario validates the audit trail fires correctly.
items.queue_note: P1 safety audit. Low effort, extremely high impact. The forbidden account rule is the most critical safety constraint in the system.

items.title: Model Activation Coverage Monitor
items.description: From 06/13 Research Drop #11: with 6 paper trading models deployed, there is no visibility into whether all models are actually activating across sessions. Some models may be silently inactive due to parameter drift, data feed issues, or scheduling gaps. Implement a model activation coverage monitor that tracks signal attempts per model per day and alerts if any model has zero activations over a configurable window.
items.source: 2026-06-13 Weekly Research Drop #11 - model activation coverage monitor
items.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN some models may be silently inactive, WHEN monitor is implemented, THEN: (1) daily log shows signal attempt count per model, (2) any model with 0 activations in a 3-session window triggers a Slack alert, (3) monitor output appears in the R8 weekly review, AND (4) inactive model alerts include the last activation timestamp for context.
items.queue_note: Requires RM-109 (session funnel) as data source. The 0 natural signals / 6 models finding makes this urgent.

items.title: Engine Reliability Scorecard as 7th Going-Live Condition
items.description: From 06/13 Research Drop #12: the current 6 going-live conditions do not include any measure of engine reliability (uptime, error rate, routine completion rate). Add a 7th going-live condition: Engine Reliability Scorecard showing >95% successful routine completions over trailing 20 sessions, <5% error rate in errors.jsonl, and all P0 sentinel checks passing.
items.source: 2026-06-13 Weekly Research Drop #12 - engine reliability as 7th going-live condition
items.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN going-live conditions do not include engine reliability, WHEN 7th condition is added, THEN: (1) FLIP_TO_LIVE.md is updated with 7th condition: Engine Reliability Scorecard, (2) scorecard thresholds are documented (>95% routine completion, <5% error rate, 0 P0 sentinel failures), (3) scorecard is auto-computed in R8 weekly review, AND (4) Jashyard explicitly approves the thresholds before they gate live trading.
items.queue_note: Update FLIP_TO_LIVE.md after Jashyard approves thresholds. Pairs with RM-101 (unit testing) and RM-105 (engine sentinel).

items.title: Public Dashboard PII Scrub Policy
items.description: From 06/13 Research Drop #13: the public dashboard may inadvertently expose PII or sensitive trading information (account balances, position sizes, P&L in dollar amounts, strategy parameters). Define and implement a PII scrub policy: document what is safe to publish vs. what must never appear, and implement scrub validation as part of the publish workflow.
items.source: 2026-06-13 Weekly Research Drop #13 - public dashboard PII scrub policy
items.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN no formal PII scrub policy exists for dashboard publishing, WHEN policy is implemented, THEN: (1) PII scrub policy document is written in TECH_NOTES.md, (2) dashboard generator validates output against the policy before any publish step, (3) any policy violation blocks publishing and alerts to Slack, AND (4) existing dashboard is audited against the policy before next publish.
items.queue_note: Pairs with RM-123 (publish_filter.py). RM-115 defines the policy; RM-123 implements technical enforcement.

items.title: FMP API Structured Retry + Exponential Backoff
items.description: From 06/13 Research Drop #14: FMP API timeouts (10 consecutive EMA-3 failures per RM-091) are handled with inconsistent retry logic. Implement structured retry with exponential backoff across all FMP API calls: configurable max retries (default 3), exponential backoff starting at 1s, a retry budget per session, and logged retry attempts and outcomes.
items.source: 2026-06-13 Weekly Research Drop #14 - FMP API structured retry + exponential backoff
items.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN FMP API calls have inconsistent retry behavior, WHEN structured retry is implemented, THEN: (1) all FMP API calls use a shared retry decorator with configurable max_retries and backoff_base, (2) each retry attempt is logged with attempt number and wait time, (3) retry budget caps total retry time per session, AND (4) exhausted retries fall back to bar-computed values (per RM-091 decision) or raise FEED_INTEGRITY_FAILURE.
items.queue_note: Pairs with RM-091 (EMA-3 promotion) and RM-110 (feed integrity gate). Implement as a shared utility decorator, not per-endpoint retry logic.

items.title: Near-Miss Score Simulation + Gate-Time Factor Snapshot
items.description: From 06/15 Research Drop #1: when the signal engine produces a near-miss score (score just below threshold), there is no record of what the score was or which factors were closest to threshold. Implement a near-miss snapshot that logs the full factor state at gate evaluation time for attempts scoring within 10% of the threshold. Enables analysis of how close we are to generating signals.
items.source: 2026-06-15 Research Drop #1 - near-miss score simulation + gate-time factor snapshot
items.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN near-miss attempts leave no analytical record, WHEN snapshot is implemented, THEN: (1) all signal attempts within 10% of threshold log a near_miss flag and full factor_scores snapshot, (2) near-miss rate is tracked in the session funnel (RM-109), (3) R8 weekly review includes a near-miss trend chart, AND (4) near-miss data feeds into gate calibration analysis.
items.queue_note: Pairs with RM-103 (factor score logging) and RM-109 (session funnel). Near-miss rate is our best proxy for market-condition readiness during signal drought.

items.title: Phase Gate Signal Floor - Phase 1 Exit Requires 15+ Valid Signals
items.description: From 06/15 Research Drop #2: the current going-live conditions have no explicit minimum signal count gate for Phase 1 completion. Define and implement a Phase Gate Signal Floor: Phase 1 cannot exit until the system has generated at least 15 valid signals (gate-passing, not just scans) in paper trading, distributed across at least 2 models. Prevents going live on near-zero signal counts.
items.source: 2026-06-15 Research Drop #2 - phase gate signal floor, Phase 1 exit requires 15+ signals
items.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN going-live conditions have no signal count floor, WHEN phase gate is implemented, THEN: (1) FLIP_TO_LIVE.md is updated: Phase 1 exit requires >=15 valid gate-passing signals, (2) current signal count is surfaced in the live readiness dashboard, (3) gate cannot be overridden without explicit Jashyard approval documented in decision_log.md, AND (4) signals from a single model are not sufficient - requires distribution across at least 2 models.
items.queue_note: DONE 2026-06-17: improvement_phases.json Phase 1 advancement_trigger replaced with compound gate — requires BOTH 10 sessions AND 15+ valid signal attempts across 2+ models. FLIP_TO_LIVE.md Step 0 added. sha256_stale flagged in MANIFEST pending on-machine rehash.

items.title: Regime Classification Tag on All Signal Attempts and Trades
items.description: From 06/15 Research Drop #4: no regime classification is attached to signal attempts or trades in the logs. Without regime tags, we cannot determine if our edge is regime-dependent. Implement a regime_tag field on all signal_attempts.jsonl and trades_paper.jsonl entries using a simple regime classifier (RSI trend, VIX level, gap magnitude).
items.source: 2026-06-15 Research Drop #4 - regime classification tag on signal attempts + trades
items.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN no regime classification exists on log entries, WHEN tagging is implemented, THEN: (1) a regime_tag enum is defined (TRENDING_UP, TRENDING_DOWN, RANGING, HIGH_VOL, LOW_VOL), (2) regime_tag is added to all new signal_attempts.jsonl and trades_paper.jsonl entries, (3) analytics can compute win rate by regime, AND (4) regime classifier is documented in TECH_NOTES.md for reproducibility.
items.queue_note: P1 analytical foundation. Pairs with RM-129 (VIX blended threshold) and RM-106 (H-VIXDECAY monitor).

items.title: Notification Fallback Chain SLA - 3-Tier: Slack to Gmail to Audit Log
items.description: From 06/15 Research Drop #7: critical notifications (trade executions, engine failures, forbidden account attempts) fail silently if Slack is unavailable. Implement a 3-tier notification fallback chain: (1) Slack primary, <30s SLA, (2) Gmail secondary, triggered after 2 Slack failures, (3) audit log entry, always written regardless of channel success.
items.source: 2026-06-15 Research Drop #7 - notification fallback chain SLA 3-tier
items.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN critical notifications fail silently when Slack is down, WHEN fallback chain is implemented, THEN: (1) all critical notification calls route through a NotificationRouter class, (2) Slack failure after 30s triggers Gmail fallback, (3) Gmail failure logs to audit_trail.jsonl, (4) audit log entry is always written regardless of channel success, AND (5) test scenario validates fallback chain when Slack is deliberately blocked.
items.queue_note: P1 live-path reliability. Slack errors must never block trading (per AI-CONTEXT). This implements that rule with a proper fallback chain.

items.title: Prod/Test Log Boundary Hard Enforcement via record_origin Field
items.description: From 06/15 Research Drop #8: production logs and test/simulation logs are not reliably separated. Test artifacts have been found in production log paths (13 errors.jsonl sim-artifacts issue from R9 cycle). Implement a record_origin field (prod/paper/sim/test) on all log writes and a hard enforcement layer that rejects writes to prod log paths from non-prod origins.
items.source: 2026-06-15 Research Drop #8 - prod/test log boundary enforcement via record_origin
items.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN test artifacts have contaminated production log paths, WHEN enforcement is implemented, THEN: (1) all log write functions accept a required record_origin parameter (prod/paper/sim/test), (2) a path guard rejects sim/test origin writes to production log paths with a hard error, (3) existing contaminated entries are identified and documented (not deleted without Jashyard approval), AND (4) path guard is tested with a deliberate sim-to-prod write attempt.
items.queue_note: P1 data integrity. Test artifact contamination corrupts gate statistics and analytics. The R9 manual relocation fix was a band-aid - this prevents recurrence.

items.title: SQQQ Dual-Threshold Conditional Entry on Confirmed Gap-Down
items.description: From 06/15 Research Drop #9: SQQQ entry logic uses the same threshold as TQQQ despite fundamentally different volatility and decay characteristics. Implement dual thresholds: a higher confidence threshold for SQQQ entries that also requires confirmation of a gap-down (QQQ opening below prior close by a configurable %). Reduces false entries on mean-reverting gap-down days.
items.source: 2026-06-15 Research Drop #9 - SQQQ dual-threshold conditional entry on confirmed gap-down
items.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN SQQQ uses same threshold as TQQQ, WHEN dual-threshold is implemented, THEN: (1) SQQQ entry requires a higher confidence score (configurable, default +10%), (2) a gap-down confirmation gate is added for SQQQ (QQQ open < prior close by configurable %, default 0.3%), (3) new thresholds run in shadow mode for 2 weeks before replacing current logic, AND (4) Jashyard reviews shadow mode results before promotion.
items.queue_note: P2 strategy refinement. Run in shadow mode first per OOS tuning rule. Do not replace current logic without paper evidence of improved SQQQ expectancy.

items.title: Public Repo Strategy Parameter Scrubbing via publish_filter.py
items.description: From 06/15 Research Drop #10: when dashboard files are pushed to the public GitHub repo, strategy parameters (score thresholds, gate values, model weights) may be embedded in HTML or JSON. Implement publish_filter.py that scrubs strategy parameters from any file before public push. The filter runs as a mandatory pre-push step and fails loudly if any strategy param pattern is detected.
items.source: 2026-06-15 Research Drop #10 - public repo strategy parameter scrubbing via publish_filter.py
items.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN strategy parameters may leak to public repo, WHEN publish_filter.py is implemented, THEN: (1) a configurable blocklist of strategy parameter patterns is defined, (2) publish_filter.py scans all files pre-push and fails with a clear error if any pattern matches, (3) filter is integrated into the Chrome upload workflow as a pre-upload check, AND (4) existing public repo files are scanned and violations documented for Jashyard review.
items.queue_note: Pairs with RM-115 (PII scrub policy). RM-115 defines the policy; RM-123 implements technical enforcement. Must ship before new strategy parameters are published.

items.title: Decision Governance Timeout + BD Staleness Gate 72h SLA
items.description: From 06/15 Research Drop #13: boardroom decisions (BD-xxx items) have no SLA for resolution. Stale decisions can accumulate and block implementation work. Implement a BD staleness gate: any BD item open for >72 hours without Jashyard response triggers an automatic escalation Slack message. After 7 days, the BD item is automatically marked ESCALATED in decision_log.md.
items.source: 2026-06-15 Research Drop #13 - decision governance timeout + BD staleness gate 72h SLA
items.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN BD items have no SLA and can sit open indefinitely, WHEN staleness gate is implemented, THEN: (1) a scheduled task checks decision_log.md daily for BD items open >72 hours, (2) Jashyard receives a Slack escalation message listing stale BD items, (3) BD items open >7 days are marked ESCALATED automatically, AND (4) staleness check is included in the R10 Saturday review.
items.queue_note: P1 governance hygiene. Stale decisions are a known bottleneck. Creates a forcing function without removing Jashyard's final authority.

items.title: afternoon_trend_v1 Forced Scan Heartbeat at R5 and R6
items.description: From 06/15 Research Drop #14: the afternoon_trend_v1 model has a 0% success rate at R7 and is not confirmed to activate at R5/R6 windows. Implement a forced scan heartbeat for afternoon_trend_v1 at R5 and R6 that confirms the model activated, logs the activation timestamp, and fires an alert if no activation detected within 5 minutes of window open.
items.source: 2026-06-15 Research Drop #14 - afternoon_trend_v1 forced scan heartbeat at R5+R6
items.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN afternoon_trend_v1 has 0% R7 success rate with unknown activation status, WHEN heartbeat is implemented, THEN: (1) R5 and R6 SKILLs include an activation confirmation check for afternoon_trend_v1, (2) activation timestamp is logged in session_log.jsonl, (3) missing activation within 5 minutes fires a Slack alert, AND (4) heartbeat data feeds RM-113 (model activation coverage monitor).
items.queue_note: Directly addresses the R7 0% mystery. Pairs with RM-107 (scheduler truth-table) and RM-113 (model activation monitor).

items.title: GitHub Sync Drift Detection via SHA-256 Comparison
items.description: From 06/15 Research Drop #16: the local codebase and GitHub remote can drift when the Chrome push workflow fails silently or is skipped. Implement a weekly GitHub sync drift detector that computes SHA-256 of key local files, compares against GitHub API file SHAs, reports any drifted files to Slack, and flags drift as a blocker for RM deployments that depend on GitHub being canonical.
items.source: 2026-06-15 Research Drop #16 - GitHub sync drift detection via SHA-256 comparison
items.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN local/GitHub drift can occur silently, WHEN drift detection is implemented, THEN: (1) weekly scheduled task compares SHA-256 of key files (signal_engine.py, SKILL.md files, MANIFEST.json) against GitHub API, (2) any drift is reported to Slack with specific file list, (3) drift blocks RM deployments touching drifted files until resolved, AND (4) MANIFEST.json SHA pins are the source of truth for drift comparison.
items.queue_note: Pairs with RM-105 (engine integrity sentinel). The sentinel checks at runtime; this detector provides a weekly out-of-band check.

items.title: v2 Model Calibration Log + Brier Pipeline Fix
items.description: From 06/15 Research Drop #17: challenger_v2 and champion_v2 models show 0 trades_all_time in the registry (confirmed in R9 recon). The Brier score pipeline for v2 models is broken or has no data. Fix: diagnose why v2 models show 0 trades_all_time, repair the Brier score pipeline, implement a calibration log tracking Brier scores per model per week, and surface calibration quality in R8 review.
items.source: 2026-06-15 Research Drop #17 - v2 model calibration log + Brier pipeline fix
items.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN v2 models show 0 trades_all_time and Brier pipeline status is unknown, WHEN fix is implemented, THEN: (1) root cause for 0 trades_all_time is identified and documented, (2) Brier score pipeline produces valid output for v2 models, (3) weekly calibration log tracks Brier scores per model, AND (4) R8 review includes a calibration quality table showing all 6 models.
items.queue_note: P1 model health. The 0 trades_all_time for v2 models was flagged in R9 recon as a registry anomaly. Must diagnose before v2 models can be trusted in live trading.

items.title: PAT Lifecycle Governance + Silent Failure Alert
items.description: From 06/15 Research Drop #21 (extends RM-003): GitHub Personal Access Tokens used for the private repo have no documented expiry, rotation schedule, or silent failure detection. If a PAT expires, the Chrome push workflow fails silently. Implement PAT lifecycle governance: document PAT expiry dates, 14-day advance expiry alert via Slack, and immediate alert on PAT failure in Chrome push workflow.
items.source: 2026-06-15 Research Drop #21 - PAT lifecycle governance + silent failure alert, extends RM-003
items.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN PAT expiry is undocumented and silent failure is possible, WHEN governance is implemented, THEN: (1) all PATs are inventoried with expiry dates in a secure location (not public repo), (2) a 14-day advance Slack alert fires before each PAT expiry, (3) Chrome push failures that may indicate PAT expiry trigger an immediate diagnostic alert, AND (4) PAT rotation procedure is documented in TECH_NOTES.md.
items.queue_note: Low effort, high security value. The 14-day advance alert is the key deliverable. Pairs with RM-126 (drift detection).

items.title: VIX Gate Blended Vol Threshold Design / H-VIXDECAY Solution Architecture
items.description: From 06/15 Research Drop #22: the VIX gate uses a single fixed threshold (30) with no regime adaptation. Design a blended volatility threshold combining VIX spot, VIX term structure (front vs. back month), and realized vol (TQQQ/SQQQ 10-day HV) into a composite vol score. Replaces the binary VIX>30 gate with a continuous vol score that adapts to current regime. Requires RM-106 (H-VIXDECAY diagnostic) data as input.
items.source: 2026-06-15 Research Drop #22 - VIX gate blended vol threshold / H-VIXDECAY solution architecture
items.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN VIX gate uses a fixed threshold with no regime adaptation, WHEN blended threshold is designed, THEN: (1) design document exists in ANALYTICS.md for the blended vol score, (2) back-test data from RM-106 feeds the design validation, (3) blended threshold runs in shadow mode alongside current VIX>30 gate for 2 weeks before any promotion, AND (4) Jashyard approves the design before shadow mode begins.
items.queue_note: P2 strategy design. GATED on RM-106 (H-VIXDECAY data). High effort; defer until P0/P1 items complete. Design first, shadow mode second, promotion only after OOS validation.

items.title: Bootstrap CI for All Small-N Gate Metrics
items.description: From 06/15 Research Drop #23: gate metrics (DSR, win rate, expectancy, Sharpe) are computed as point estimates with no confidence intervals. With small sample sizes (n<30), these estimates have wide uncertainty bands that are invisible to the decision-maker. Implement bootstrap confidence intervals (95% CI via resampling) for all key gate metrics and surface CIs in the R8 review and live readiness dashboard.
items.source: 2026-06-15 Research Drop #23 - bootstrap CI for all small-N gate metrics
items.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN gate metrics are point estimates with no uncertainty quantification, WHEN bootstrap CI is implemented, THEN: (1) all key metrics (win rate, expectancy, DSR, Sharpe) show 95% bootstrap CI alongside point estimate, (2) CIs are displayed in R8 weekly review and live readiness dashboard, (3) CI widths flag metrics with insufficient data (wide CI = unreliable), AND (4) going-live condition 1 (win rate) requires the lower CI bound to meet the threshold, not just the point estimate.
items.queue_note: P1 statistical rigor. Currently making decisions on metrics that may be statistically indistinguishable from random with n<10. Bootstrap CI makes uncertainty explicit. Pairs with RM-108 (DSR de-hardcode) and KPI audit findings.

items.title: Eliminate Chrome MCP Dependency from All Entry Routines (Unattended Execution Fix)
items.description: P0 reliability fix. On 2026-06-17, R1 missed its 9:43 ET window due to this. Fix: replace Chrome send with draft_only pattern across R1/R2/R3/R4/R5/R6. GitHub MCP push_files tested and confirmed 403 — Chrome UI remains sole GitHub push path; documented in R2 SKILL. R7 flagged for follow-up.
items.source: 2026-06-17 R1 missed window — Chrome approval dialog blocked unattended scheduled task
items.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN entry routines use Chrome MCP for email send which blocks unattended execution, WHEN fix is applied, THEN: (1) R1/R2/R3/R4/R5/R6 SKILL.md files contain zero mcp__Claude_in_Chrome__* calls in email-send sections, (2) email_status='draft_only' is the documented success state, (3) Slack is primary real-time channel, (4) GitHub MCP 403 documented in R2 SKILL, AND (5) QA grep confirms zero Chrome blocking calls in entry routine email sections.
items.queue_note: DONE 2026-06-17: P0 fix executed same session as detection. R7 Chrome email dep — follow-up RM pending. GitHub MCP push_files = 403; Chrome UI sole push path.

items.title: FOMC R5 Gate Extension: Extend No-Entry Gate to Cover R5 on FOMC Days
items.description: Hypothesis H-FOMC-R5: trend model entries at R5 (12:30 ET) on FOMC decision days carry asymmetric downside risk — entries pre-positioned before 14:00 announcement are exposed to announcement-bar stops with no exit opportunity. Current gate (BD-20260613-04) covers R6 only. Evidence: 2026-06-17 — 3 R5 entries (champion_v2, challenger_v2, afternoon_trend_v1) all stopped at 14:00 bar, -$14.17 total. Scope: validate against full FOMC calendar in event_calendar.json, then extend FOMC flag suppression in signal_engine.py R5 window for trend models. Scalp models with time-exits evaluated separately.
items.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN H-FOMC-R5 hypothesis, WHEN validated against FOMC calendar (N>=3 FOMC days), THEN extend gate to R5 for trend models in signal_engine.py. FOMC days produce zero R5 trend entries. Scalp model decision documented.
